title: ranunculus cardiophyllus var. subsagittatus
intro: Ranunculus cardiophyllus var. subsagittatus, also known as the Mountain Buttercup, is a perennial plant native to mountainous regions. The plant contains alkaloids, particularly ranunculin and protoanemonin, which have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Ranunculus cardiophyllus var. subsagittatus include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural antiseptic to prevent infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried or made into a tincture, and its extracts are used in topical applications for wound care and skin conditions.
